iterations.strategy: Root cause: the current COUNT(DISTINCT adsh) path materializes one packed (group,adsh) key per input row and then sorts/uniques it twice, so the hot phases spend most of their time moving and sorting about 9.6M 64-bit keys (76.8 MB raw, with thread-local plus global copies) even though the GROUP BY domain is tiny. Strategy category B: replace the cache-unfriendly sort-based distinct with a cache-resident direct-array plan. Aggregate COUNT(*) and SUM(line) in a 27-slot direct array indexed by (stmt_code,rfile_code), and maintain exact DISTINCT state as a thread-local dense uint32_t mask array over the 86,136-code global adsh domain; each row ORs its group bit into mask[adsh]. Finalization reduces thread-local arrays by sum/OR and computes num_filings by popcounting the reduced masks, eliminating the large sort/unique phases and keeping the working set inside cache.
